Both implementations iterate from index 0, squaring elements at indices multiple of 3, cubing at indices multiple of 4 but not 3, leaving others unchanged, and summing the results. Python int and Coq Z arithmetic align for these operations.

Both use 0-based indexing and identical modulo logic (square if index%3==0, else cube if index%4==0 and not %3==0, else unchanged), summing results. Integers are unbounded in both, and pre/spec match equality.

Both start index at 0 and for each element: square if index%3==0, else cube if index%4==0 and not %3==0, else keep; sum all results. Python ints match Coq Z arithmetic, so outputs align on all inputs.
